name: EnergyCAP Utility Bill Management API
what_it_does: EnergyCAP Utility Bill Management REST API for enterprise utility expense tracking, energy management, and sustainability reporting. Enables AI agents to manage utility bill processing and accounts payable automation for energy expense management, handle energy benchmark and performance tracking for facility sustainability, access carbon emissions calculation and scope 1/2/3 reporting for ESG compliance, retrieve energy budget and variance analysis for cost management, manage renewable energy certificate (REC) tracking for clean energy compliance, handle utility contract and rate management for procurement optimization, access energy efficiency project tracking for ROI measurement, retrieve meter and submeter data management for granular energy analytics, manage ENERGY STAR certification and benchmarking for building performance, and integrate EnergyCAP with ERP, building automation, and sustainability reporting platforms.
use_cases: Processing utility bills from AP automation agents
Tracking carbon emissions from ESG reporting agents
Benchmarking energy performance from sustainability agents
Managing REC portfolios from clean energy agents
not_for: Small businesses without multi-meter utility complexity
Residential energy management without enterprise context
Real-time energy monitoring without utility bill focus
best_when: A large organization (corporate real estate, government, university, healthcare) wants AI agents to automate utility bill processing, energy benchmarking, carbon emissions tracking, and ESG sustainability reporting.
avoid_when: SEC CLIMATE DISCLOSURE ACCURACY: Automated carbon emissions calculations for SEC climate disclosure (proposed rules) or voluntary ESG reporting (GHG Protocol) must follow defined methodology and be subject to third-party verification; automated scope calculations with incorrect emission factors or system boundaries create material ESG disclosure inaccuracy. Utility bill processing financial controls — automated utility bill payment must maintain financial controls (three-way match, budget approval); automated payment of incorrect or fraudulent utility bills creates financial loss and AP compliance risk. REC and carbon offset verification — automated renewable energy claim and carbon offset tracking must verify instrument validity and retirement; unverified REC or offset claims create greenwashing liability under FTC Green Guides.

auth.notes: EnergyCAP uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.energycap.com. State College, Pennsylvania HQ. Founded 1982. Private (Renovus Capital Partners). 5,000+ organizations. Utility bill management and energy accounting leader. Competes with Watchwire, Urjanet, and Schneider Electric for utility bill management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-meter or per-facility annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-meter or enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: State College, Pennsylvania. Renovus Capital-backed. Founded 1982. 5,000+ organizations. Utility bill management. Competes with Watchwire and Schneider for energy management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; bulk utility bill processing endpoints available

known_agent_gotchas: ESG DISCLOSURE ACCURACY: Automated GHG emissions calculations for SEC climate disclosure or GHG Protocol reporting must use correct emission factors and system boundary definitions; automated scope 2 calculations using incorrect emission factors create material ESG disclosure inaccuracy requiring restatement

Utility bill AP controls — automated utility bill payment must maintain AP financial controls; implement three-way match (invoice, expected bill, budget) before automated payment; uncontrolled automated payment of fraudulent or incorrect bills creates financial loss

REC and carbon offset greenwashing risk — automated renewable energy and carbon offset claims must verify REC retirement in WREGIS/ERCOT and offset verification in CDP/Gold Standard; unverified green claims violate FTC Green Guides and create greenwashing liability

Utility billing error detection — automated bill processing must flag anomalies (unusual consumption, rate errors, billing period errors); automated payment of bills with processing errors creates overpayment and utility dispute burden

ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager data quality — automated ENERGY STAR benchmarking data must meet EPA data quality requirements; inaccurate floor area, operating hours, or occupancy data creates incorrect ENERGY STAR score

Multi-commodity energy management — automated energy management for electricity, natural gas, water, and steam requires commodity-specific data models and emission factors; mixed commodity automation without proper categorization creates reporting errors
agent_readiness.security_notes: Energy management. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Utility and energy consumption data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Energy management. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Utility and energy data.
